description: Table monumod du mod TCP (colonnes documentees via le D2R Data Guide).
source: Colonnes du .txt (mod TCP) ; types/descriptions du D2R Data Guide (guide/).

columns.name: uniquemod
columns.description: This is a reference field to define the monster modifier

columns.name: id
columns.description: Defines the unique numeric ID for the monster modifier. Used as a reference in other data files

columns.name: enabled
columns.description: If equals 1, then this monster modifier will be an available option for monsters to spawn with. If equals 0, then this monster modifier will never be used

columns.name: version
columns.description: Defines which game version to use this monster modifier (<100 = Classic mode | 100 = Expansion mode)

columns.name: xfer
columns.description: If equals 1, then this monster modifier can be transferred from the Boss monster to his Minion monsters, including auras. If equals 0, then the monster modifier will never be transferred

columns.name: champion
columns.description: If equals 1, then this monster modifier will only be used by Champion monsters. If equals 0, then the monster modifier can be used by any type of special monster

columns.name: fPick
columns.description: Controls if this monster modifier is allowed on the monster based on the function code and the parameters it checks.

columns.name: constants
columns.description: These values control a special list of numeric parameters for special monsters. The row that each constant appears in the data file is unrelated. You can treat this column almost like a separate data file that controls other aspects of special monsters. See the description next to each value for more specific clarification on each constant.
